title: How a BFSI SaaS Company dominated AI search in 90 days
description: From near-zero visibility to ranking in Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, and featured snippets all through strategic content SEO
content:
The challenge: invisible in a noisy market


A lending and ICM (Incentive Compensation Management) SaaS platform for the BFSI sector had strong product capabilities but weak organic search presence. Competitors were running paid ads on high-intent keywords, while the company had little to no content footprint.


The goal: build a content-led SEO strategy that could cut through paid competition and earn a seat at the table in the emerging world of AI-generated search answers.


Content built for humans and AI models


The SEO strategy prioritised what we call "AI-citation-worthy" content, long-form, well-structured articles targeting high-intent queries in the BFSI space that AI systems like Google's AI Overviews and ChatGPT actively pull from when answering user queries.

Content was produced targeting BoFU (Bottom of Funnel) keywords where competitors spent money on ads but left organic results wide open, a direct content arbitrage play.


On the BoFU keyword targeting front, we identified competitor ad spend keywords with no strong organic content competition. Published authoritative long-form content to capture the organic slot they were paying to skip.

For Malaysia market expansion, we launched Malaysia-focused blog content in Q4 2025. By October, 15 active Malaysian users appeared, the first meaningful international foothold for the brand.


On AI Overview optimisation, we structured content for Google's AI Overviews with clear definitions, structured headings, and authoritative coverage, leading to multiple AI Overview citations from August onward.


ChatGPT.com began referring sessions to the company starting August 2025, with a consistent upward trend through October, 31 sessions tracked in the period.


Appearing where buyers are asking questions


One of the most significant outcomes of the campaign was earning citations inside AI-generated answers, a channel that traditional SEO rarely accounts for. The company's content now surfaces inside Google AI Overviews and ChatGPT responses for high-intent BFSI queries.



Articles began appearing as cited sources inside AI-generated answers on both Google and ChatGPT, a direct result of authoritative, well-structured content targeting niche BFSI queries. ChatGPT referral sessions went from zero to 31 between August and October 2025. Over 10 keywords were featured in Google AI Overviews.


Content was ranking #1 for queries like "stp credit," "what is bfsi sales," and "zero code lending platform," while also appearing in the AI Overview panel on those same results pages.


Positions earned in a competitive BFSI landscape


Brand terms achieved position #1, while high-intent informational and commercial queries climbed into top 1-5 positions across Sep-Oct 2025.



Steady compounding from April to October 2025


Traffic and user growth accelerated significantly after the SEO programme launched in August, with October 2025 reaching the highest monthly totals on record, 1,500 active users and 3,400 views.



Organic search views grew from 398 in April to 783 in October, nearly doubling in 6 months without any paid media.


A blueprint for AI-era SEO in BFSI


In just three months of active SEO execution, the company achieved what many fintech brands spend years and significant paid budgets to accomplish: consistent top-of-SERP visibility, AI citation coverage, and a growing organic traffic engine across two markets.


The results validate a clear hypothesis: well-structured, deeply authoritative content targeting the right niche queries doesn't just rank on Google. In 2025, it gets cited by AI. That's a compounding asset no ad spend can replicate.


All key metrics showed strong year-on-year growth. User base, engagement, and retention significantly improved, with the brand now appearing in AI-generated answers across Google and ChatGPT for high-value BFSI search queries.

title: From Traffic to Pipeline: 60 Sales-Ready Leads in 60 Days
description:
How Gushwork.ai turned stalled top-of-funnel traffic into a pipeline of Founders, CEOs, and Directors through precision LinkedIn Ads across 5 geographies in two months.

Channel: LinkedIn Ads (Lead Gen)
Duration: Oct - Dec 2024 (60 days)
Industry: B2B
content:
Good product. Great traffic. Zero pipeline.


Gushwork.ai, an AI-first platform built for staffing and recruitment agencies, was seeing solid website traffic, but conversions weren't keeping up. Most visitors were still in the awareness or consideration stage, interested, but not quite ready to take the next step.


That's where GTXO Consulting stepped in. Over 60 days we ran a highly targeted LinkedIn Ads programme, constantly testing and refining campaigns across geographies, audiences, and creatives. The focus was simple: reach real decision-makers and drive meaningful conversations.



Four compounding problems blocking pipeline growth



Our approach: built to test fast, scale what worked, kill what didn't


The playbook had three phases: define the audience precisely, launch structured multi-geo campaigns, then iterate aggressively based on CPL performance, pausing losers within days, doubling down on winners.






Month 1, October 2024: experimental India campaign


Before scaling budget or geography, we ran a single experimental campaign targeting India exclusively to validate the core hypothesis, calibrate CPL expectations, and test creative messaging with the ICP. It delivered 25 leads at $29 CPL in 30 days. All decision-makers. 30,357 impressions with a 1.06% CTR.


Month 2, November 2024: full geo scale-up


Budget scaled 4x and expanded into US, UK, Canada, and ANZ alongside India. Seven campaigns ran simultaneously. The broader geo mix raised the blended CPL to $88, expected at scale, while delivering 35 additional leads, all decision-makers. 5 geographies running simultaneously across India, US, UK, Canada, and ANZ. Every lead was a Founder, CEO, MD, or Director.


Lead quality: every single lead was a decision-maker



Key learnings: what worked and what we cut


What worked: phone capture over email-only for India. Founder/CEO messaging converts best. Rapid A/B testing to find winning angles. ICP-fit leads convert to pipeline faster.


What we optimised: low-ROI geos killed within 2 weeks. Budget reallocated to best performers. Bid optimization to reduce CPL. Dormant leads moved to nurture tracks.


A proven playbook for B2B pipeline on LinkedIn


In 60 days, Gushwork.ai went from a top-heavy funnel with no paid engine to 60 sales-ready leads, every one of them a C-suite or Director-level buyer at a recruitment or staffing firm. The CPL dropped 50% month-over-month as we cut losing campaigns and scaled winning creatives.


The result validates a clear formula: precise ICP targeting + structured A/B testing + ruthless budget reallocation = a scalable, predictable pipeline engine on LinkedIn.


"Abhirup proved to be an exceptional partner for our GTM initiatives. His expertise in executing our paid marketing experiments accelerated our testing cycles significantly. Through his partnership, we were able to generate a substantial sales-qualified pipeline in a short time frame."


Swapnil Sinha, GTM Director, Gushwork.ai, January 2025

title: From High CPL to 5.5x ROAS in 13 Weeks
cover_glyph: 5.5x ROAS with 3x revenue growth in 13 Weeks
description: Scaling a D2C Astrology Product on Meta Ads, 3x revenue growth in 4 months
